topic: A dancer performing the 'Haka' with correct 'Pukana' facial expressions.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Dancer enters frame in haka stance, front view.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Dancer stomps and raises arms while beginning Pukana, side-angle.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: Dancer's face fills frame in full Pukana with wide eyes and tongue protruded, close-up.
parsed_generation.narration: An eight-second sequence follows a single dancer delivering a haka with authentic Pukana intensity. The shots move from a frontal stance to dynamic arm stomps, culminating in a piercing close-up of the facial expressions.

feedback: The gold standard shows a group performing in unison and a two-shot structure with a strong group-wide opening before a close-up; the generated output renders a single dancer and a three-shot sequence. To align more closely, adopt a two-part structure with [00:00-00:04] establishing the group in haka posture and [00:04-00:08] a close-up on pukana, plus explicit unison stomping or chest-slapping cues. Enhance visual richness with lighting, textures, and clearer camera moves (wide, tracking) to match the cinematic, action-first style of the gold standard.
strengths: Clear, action-forward shot labeling and camera-angle cues (front view, side-angle, close-up).
Explicit focus on pukana facial expressions in the close-up.
fixes: Introduce a group-scene framing to reflect unison haka performance (or explicitly state multiple dancers).
Revise the shot breakdown to mirror the gold standard’s 0–4s and 4–8s cadence and enhance visual detail (lighting, textures, and dynamic camera moves such as tracking).
